findings.title: ScreenshotCapture missing error propagation test
findings.description: ScreenshotCapture.capture() wraps an external provider call but there is no test verifying behavior when the provider throws an error. Since this is an Imperative Shell boundary wrapping a platform API (Electron desktopCapturer), error propagation is a critical behavioral path. If the provider fails (permission denied, display unavailable, etc.), the caller needs to know how the error surfaces. Currently, the error would propagate as an unhandled rejection with no transformation or context enrichment.

findings.title: transitionEmotion missing test for unknown trigger name
findings.description: The emotion-state-machine.test.ts file tests transitions for 'rest-reminder' and the no-trigger case, but does not test the fallback behavior when a trigger fires with an unknown triggerName (one not in TRIGGER_EMOTION_MAP). The source code uses nullish coalescing (??) to fall back to currentState, but this boundary is unverified. As more triggers are added, this fallback path becomes increasingly important to prevent silent state corruption.

findings.title: generateResponse missing test for unknown trigger/default fallback
findings.description: The response-generator tests only cover the 'rest-reminder' trigger with known emotion states. There is no test for: (1) an unknown triggerName that falls through to DEFAULT_MESSAGE, or (2) a known triggerName with an emotion state that has no template but falls through to the neutral template. The generateResponse function has a three-level fallback chain (exact match -> neutral template -> DEFAULT_MESSAGE) but only the first level is tested.

findings.title: calculateContinuousWorkDuration no test for unsorted input
findings.description: The function's JSDoc states events must be sorted ascending by timestamp, but there is no defensive guard in the implementation and no test documenting what happens with unsorted input. If events are passed in arbitrary order, the function silently computes an incorrect duration (potentially negative gaps). This is a precondition that should either be enforced (sort or throw) or tested to document the contract explicitly.

findings.title: evaluateTriggers missing multi-trigger priority test
findings.code_snippet:
const triggers = [T03_REST_REMINDER]

it('returns triggered=true when a condition is met and not in cooldown', () => {
findings.description: The evaluateTriggers tests only use a single trigger (T03_REST_REMINDER). The function iterates over an array of triggers and returns the first match, establishing an implicit priority based on array order. There is no test verifying this priority behavior when multiple triggers could match simultaneously. While this is acceptable for the walking skeleton (only one trigger exists), it should be addressed as new triggers are added.

positive_observations: Test doubles for external APIs include proper rationale comments as required by CLAUDE.md (screenshot.test.ts line 6-7)
Functional Core modules (duration, proactive-trigger, emotion-state-machine, response-generator) are tested with direct instantiation and pure input/output - no mocks on domain logic
End-to-end pipeline test in persona-engine.test.ts validates the full trigger->emotion->response flow using real module imports, proving real collaboration without mocks
Boundary conditions are well-covered: empty arrays, single elements, exact boundary values (TWO_HOURS_MS exactly), cooldown edge cases (just past cooldown), and custom thresholds
Test helper functions (makeEvent, makeTriggerInput, makeTriggered) provide clean factory patterns without introducing mock/fake abstractions
No forbidden patterns detected: no jest.fn(), no jest.mock(), no InMemory/Mock/Fake repository classes, no skipped tests
